differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy of the affected regions
Dermatologic examination with dermoscopy to exclude occult cutaneous melanoma
Whole-body PET-CT or MRI to search for occult primary malignancy
Immunohistochemical staining of biopsy tissue for melanocytic markers (S100, HMB-45, Melan-A, MITF)
Molecular testing for GNAQ or GNA11 mutations
Repeat cerebrospinal fluid analysis for cytology, flow cytometry, and malignant markers (e.g., 5-S-cysteinyldopa)
